definition: Compare individuals using genome-wide allele-sharing measures such as identity-by-state (IBS), identity-by-descent (IBD), kinship, or relatedness coefficients. For SPECTRA, this audits whether genetic prediction performance depends on evaluation genomes being close to training genomes by ancestry, family relationship, or shared haplotypes.
example_usage: python genotype_ibs_similarity.py --train train_genotypes.csv --eval eval_genotypes.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --marker-cols-prefix snp_

citations.title: Inference of Relationships in Population Data Using Identity-by-Descent and Identity-by-State

evidence.summary: The paper describes relatedness assessment using IBD and IBS allele-sharing proportions over SNP genotype data.
It reports IBS and IBD methods for population structure, outliers, and pairwise relationships.

limitations: Naive IBS can be confounded by ancestry, genotyping platform, linkage disequilibrium, and missingness.
IBD estimation may require phasing, windows, or population-specific calibration.
Genetic relatedness is sensitive and may require privacy-preserving handling.
quality_gates: Report genotype encoding, marker filtering, missingness thresholds, LD pruning, and ancestry handling.
Separate family relatedness from population-structure similarity when possible.
Use established tools for production IBD/kinship estimates; the bundled script is a simple dosage IBS example.
